summary: Linked Kirby Sheik copy-special (`ftkirbyspecialseak.c`) by splitting the trailing Mewtwo copy-special code out into a new nonmatching `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`/`.h`, updating splits/symbols/build config, and cleaning shared Kirby SpecialN callback inline naming/includes. The PR had no body or review comments, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.

key_files.role: Now matching; reduced to Sheik-copy-special code by removing the Mewtwo-copy-special tail and unnecessary includes.
New nonmatching file containing the extracted Kirby Mewtwo copy-special implementation from 0x80106F44-0x80109290.
New header for `ftKb_SpecialNMt_*` and `ftKb_MtSpecial*` prototypes formerly sitting in `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`.
Removed unrelated Mewtwo-copy-special prototypes from the Donkey header.
New shared Kirby inline header defining `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs` for death/take-damage callback setup.
Split `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` text/data/sdata2 ranges and inserted `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` ranges.
Adjusted data/sdata2 symbols around the split, making several former `ftKb_Init_*` globals local anonymous labels.
Changed `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` from NonMatching to Matching and added `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` as NonMatching.
Updated include dependency from `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` to the new Mewtwo-specific Kirby header.

what_changed: `configure.py` now builds `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialseak.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)` and adds `melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` as `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` shortened `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` text from `0x80105FEC-0x80109290` to `0x80105FEC-0x80106F44`, gave it data `0x803CB770-0x803CB828` and sdata2 `0x804D94F8-0x804D9508`, and created a new `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` split for text `0x80106F44-0x80109290`, rodata `0x803B7570-0x803B7588`, and sdata2 `0x804D9508-0x804D9554`.

Moved Mewtwo copy-special functions such as `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80106F44`, `ftKb_MtSpecialNStart_Anim`, `ftKb_MtSpecialNLoop_IASA`, and related Phys/Coll handlers out of `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` into new `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`.
Created `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.h` and moved the Mewtwo-copy-special prototypes out of `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`.

Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/inlines.h` with shared `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`, then updated several Kirby copy-special files to use that clearer callback-setting inline name instead of `ftKirbyDmgInline`.
Updated `itmewtwoshadowball.c` to include `ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.h` instead of the unrelated Donkey header.
Made Mewtwo rodata vectors `ftKb_Init_803B7570` and `ftKb_Init_803B757C` static const in the new Mewtwo file, and removed their externs from Zelda copy-special code.
Changed several split-adjacent data/sdata2 symbols from global `ftKb_Init_*` names to local anonymous labels such as `@304`, `@352`, `@249`, `@257`, and `@480`.
smart_moves: Used a file-boundary split at `0x80106F44` to isolate the already-matchable Sheik portion from the still-nonmatching Mewtwo portion, allowing partial linking progress without solving all remaining code at once.
Kept build status explicit: the newly cleaned `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` became Matching, while the extracted `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` stayed NonMatching.
Introduced a Mewtwo-specific header and updated item code to include it, removing misleading coupling through `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h`.
Centralized the common Kirby copy-special callback setup as `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`, avoiding repeated ambiguous `ftKirbyDmgInline` helpers across copy-special files.
Adjusted symbols and split ranges together, including localizing data/sdata2 labels, which is important when moving code/data ownership between source files.
decomp_lessons: For large copy-special files that contain multiple copied character implementations, split by address/system boundary to mark the matching subsection immediately and leave the unsolved tail in a new nonmatching object.
When moving a code block to a new source file, move its prototypes into a semantically correct header and fix downstream includes; do not leave unrelated declarations in a convenient older header.
Shared callback setup helpers should be named for behavior and owning subsystem (`ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`) rather than vague damage-related names (`ftKirbyDmgInline`).
After a file split, verify `.text`, `.rodata`, `.data`, and `.sdata2` ranges, not just `.text`; constants and local data often move ownership with the extracted code.
If data symbols become file-local after a split, represent that in `symbols.txt` with local labels rather than keeping artificial globals.
Include cleanup can be part of matching/linking work: `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` dropped Mewtwo, Ice Climbers, lb, collision, and parts headers once the Mewtwo code was extracted.
naming_conventions: Kirby copy-special files use `ftkirbyspecial<character>.c` lowercase file names, e.g. `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` and new `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c`.
Kirby Mewtwo-copy-special functions use the `ftKb_SpecialNMt_*` and `ftKb_MtSpecial*_{Anim,IASA,Phys,Coll}` prefixes.
Shared Kirby SpecialN callback setup was renamed from `ftKirbyDmgInline` to `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`; a two-callback variant remains as `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs2` in `ftKb_SpecialN.c`.
Header ownership should follow the subsystem: Mewtwo copy-special declarations moved from `ftkirbyspecialdonkey.h` to `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.h`.
The new Mewtwo header uses an address-style include guard `GALE01_106F44`, matching the file start address of the extracted block.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted `ftkirbyspecialseak.c` from NonMatching to Matching by extracting the nonmatching Mewtwo code rather than trying to force all of `0x80105FEC-0x80109290` to match as one source file.
Preserved exact split boundaries: Sheik text ends at `0x80106F44`, Mewtwo text starts at `0x80106F44`, preventing overlap/gaps.
Moved rodata vectors at `0x803B7570` and `0x803B757C` into the new Mewtwo file as `static Vec3 const`, aligning constant ownership with the functions that use them.
Localized data and sdata2 symbols in `symbols.txt` after ownership changed, e.g. `ftKb_Init_803CB794` became local `@304` and `ftKb_Init_804D94F8` became local `@249`.
Used a shared inline header to keep the callback-setting code available to multiple translation units while preserving inlining behavior needed by matching code.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice; there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that link `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.c` or change `ftKb_SpecialNMt_*` functions.
Search for remaining `ftKirbyDmgInline` helpers and replace with behavior-specific `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs` where appropriate.
Audit Kirby copy-special headers for declarations living in unrelated character headers.
Check whether `ftkirbyspecialmewtwo.h` include guard `GALE01_106F44` was later renamed to a path-based guard.
Review other large Kirby copy-special files for opportunities to split matching subranges from nonmatching tails.
